mnemonics: Arrays of Objects: A Box for Many Objects
Pointers to Objects: Pointing to a Specific Object
Pointers to Derived Type: Inheriting Pointers
keyPoints: Arrays of Objects are used to store multiple objects of the same class
Pointers to Objects are used to access and modify the data of an object
The 'this' pointer is a reference to the current object
Pointers to derived type are used to access the base class members of a derived class object
Arrays of Objects can be used to store a collection of objects of the same class
Pointers to Objects can be used to create a copy of an object
The 'this' pointer is used to call member functions of the current object
Pointers to derived type can be used to access the base class members of a derived class object
Arrays of Objects can be used to store objects of different classes
Pointers to Objects can be used to create a pointer to an object of a different class
The 'this' pointer is used to access the data members of the current object
Pointers to derived type can be used to access the base class members of a derived class object
